title: oreochloa disticha subsp. pedemontana
intro: Oreochloa disticha subsp. pedemontana, also known as the Italian red fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Oreochloa disticha subsp. pedemontana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate joint pain, and improve kidney function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
